data.title: French Economic Indicators Decline Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: French industry and consumer confidence have seen a decline, with households expressing increased concerns about inflation following the outbreak of the Iran war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on economic sentiment in France. The conflict has led to uncertainty, affecting both industrial output and consumer spending. As inflation expectations rise, households are becoming more cautious, potentially influencing future economic activity. The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global events and their influence on national economies.

data.title: India Seeks Assistance to Mitigate LPG Shortage Impact on Steel Industry
data.description: India's Ministry of Steel has approached the Ministry of Petroleum for assistance to ensure that steel plants are not adversely affected by a shortage of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), according to sources. India, the world's second-largest producer of crude steel, is currently facing its most severe LPG supply crisis in decades due to the conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, a source stated, "We have communicated with the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to explore the most effective measures under the current conditions. Discussions are ongoing." Earlier reports indicated that small steel producers in India have warned of potential production halts due to the gas shortage.

data.title: Binance Alpha: The threshold for claiming the TRADOR airdrop is 242 points, and claiming it will consume 15 points.
data.description: Binance Alpha's second Tradoor (TRADOOR) airdrop has begun. Users need to hold at least 242 Alpha Points to participate and will receive 14 TRADOOR tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully allocated, the points threshold will decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Alpha Points, and users must confirm on the event page within 24 hours; otherwise, they will be considered to have forfeited their claim.

data.title: Currys CEO Alex Baldock to Step Down After Eight Years
data.description: Currys CEO Alex Baldock is set to resign after leading the electricals retailer for eight years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Baldock's role in revitalizing the company during his tenure. His departure marks the end of a significant era for Currys, as he played a crucial part in the company's turnaround. The search for his successor is underway, with the company aiming to maintain its positive trajectory.

data.title: France's March INSEE Business Confidence Index Matches Previous Value
data.description: France's March INSEE Business Confidence Index remained steady at 97, aligning with the previous month's figure, according to Jin10. The index slightly exceeded expectations, which were set at 96, indicating a stable business sentiment in the country. The consistency in the index suggests that French businesses are maintaining confidence despite broader economic uncertainties.

data.title: U.S. Court Dismisses Legal Protection Suit for Crypto Tool Pharos
data.description: A U.S. court has dismissed a lawsuit filed by Michael Lewellen seeking legal protection for Pharos, a non-custodial cryptocurrency tool. According to NS3.AI, the Northern District of Texas ruled that Lewellen did not demonstrate a substantial threat of prosecution, a necessary condition for a pre-enforcement challenge. The court also cited a Department of Justice memo advising prosecutors against pursuing crypto services for end-user actions or unintentional violations. The dismissal was issued without prejudice, allowing Lewellen the option to refile if the risk of enforcement becomes more imminent.

data.title: 4.7 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Off Aomori Prefecture, Japan
data.description: A 4.7 magnitude earthquake occurred off the eastern coast of Aomori Prefecture, Japan, at approximately 16:37 local time on March 26, according to Jin10. The Japan Meteorological Agency reported that the earthquake's maximum intensity was recorded at level 3, with a depth of 40 kilometers. There is no tsunami risk associated with this seismic event.

data.title: Indonesia Allows Batang Toru Hydropower Plant to Resume Operations
data.description: Indonesia has granted permission for the Batang Toru Hydropower Plant in North Sumatra to resume operations, according to Jin10. This decision comes after a period of halted activities at the facility. The hydropower plant is a significant project in the region, contributing to Indonesia's renewable energy goals. The resumption of operations is expected to bolster local energy supply and support the country's sustainable development initiatives.

data.title: The X platform is gradually returning to normal, after experiencing five large-scale outages in the past three months.
data.description: Community feedback indicates that the X platform is gradually returning to normal. Downdetector data shows that the platform has experienced five significant outages or widespread disruptions in the past three months.

data.title: X Platform Gradually Recovers After Multiple Outages
data.description: X Platform is reportedly returning to normal operations following recent disruptions. According to BlockBeats, community feedback indicates that the platform is gradually recovering. Downdetector records show that X Platform has experienced five significant outages or widespread interruptions over the past three months.

data.title: CBOE Volatility Index Rises Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description: The Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE) Volatility Index has experienced an increase of 1.17 points, reaching a current level of 26.5 points. According to ChainCatcher, this rise reflects ongoing market fluctuations and investor sentiment. The index, often referred to as the "fear gauge," is a key measure of market expectations of near-term volatility conveyed by S&P 500 stock index option prices. This uptick suggests heightened uncertainty in the financial markets.

data.title: Next Faces Increased Costs Due to Middle East Conflict
data.description: Retailer Next has announced that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is expected to increase its costs by £15 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this development comes despite the company raising its profit guidance. The additional expenses are anticipated to impact the retailer's financial performance, overshadowing the positive adjustments to its profit expectations. Next continues to navigate the challenges posed by geopolitical tensions while maintaining its business operations.

data.title: Social Media Platform 'X' Faces Issues in the U.S., Reports DownDetector
data.description: According to Jin10, DownDetector, a website that monitors network conditions, has reported user complaints indicating problems with the social media platform 'X' in the United States. The issues have led to disruptions in service, affecting user experience across the platform. DownDetector's data suggests a significant number of users have encountered difficulties accessing features or experiencing delays. The platform's technical team is reportedly working to resolve the issues and restore normal functionality. Users are advised to stay updated through official channels for further information on the situation.

data.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards: Houthis Ready to Control Strategic Strait
data.description: On March 26, according to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, informed sources revealed that Yemen's Houthi forces are prepared to take control of the strategically significant Mandeb Strait. According to Jin10, the source stated that the Houthis have been on high alert since the first day of U.S. and Israeli actions against Iran, ready to join the battlefield at any moment. The source emphasized that if necessary, the Houthis are fully prepared to play a crucial role by controlling the Mandeb Strait to further punish adversaries. Additionally, the source highlighted that only Iran possesses the capability to pose a credible threat in the Mandeb Strait. He noted that the Houthis have demonstrated their ability to easily blockade the Mandeb Strait and enforce the resistance movement's will in the Red Sea.

data.title: France's Trade Minister: Strategic Oil Reserve Release to be Discussed at G7 Meeting Next Monday
data.description: France's Trade Minister has announced that the release of strategic oil reserves will be a topic of discussion at the upcoming G7 ministerial meeting scheduled for next Monday. According to Jin10, this meeting will bring together leaders from the Group of Seven nations to address global energy concerns and potential strategies for stabilizing oil markets. The discussion comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and fluctuating oil prices, highlighting the importance of coordinated international efforts to manage energy resources effectively. The release of strategic reserves is seen as a potential measure to alleviate market pressures and ensure energy security. The G7 meeting will provide a platform for member countries to explore collaborative approaches to these challenges.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds $70K as ETF Inflows Hit $11.3B, Institutional Demand Offsets Retail Selling
data.description: Key TakeawaysBitcoin ETFs recorded 62,986 BTC inflows (~$11.3B) over the past 30 days, pushing total holdings to 1.327M BTC.Short-term holders are still selling at a loss, with ~15,500 BTC daily inflows to exchanges.Institutional demand is absorbing retail selling pressure, supporting Bitcoin’s price.Analysts say BTC must hold above $70,000 and sustain ETF inflows to maintain the uptrend.Bitcoin is holding above the $70,000 level as strong institutional inflows continue to offset persistent selling pressure from short-term holders, highlighting a growing divergence in market behavior.According to crypto analyst Axel, Bitcoin ETFs recorded a net inflow of 62,986 BTC—worth approximately $11.3 billion—between Feb. 24 and March 25. Total ETF holdings have now reached 1.327 million BTC, underscoring sustained institutional accumulation.At the same time, short-term holders continue to exit positions at a loss. Exchange data shows an average of 15,500 BTC per day flowing into exchanges from loss-making participants, indicating ongoing capitulation among retail traders.This dynamic suggests that institutional buyers are increasingly absorbing available supply, helping stabilize Bitcoin’s price despite weak sentiment among shorter-term market participants.The trend reflects a broader shift in market structure, where ETF-driven demand is playing a larger role in price support compared to previous cycles dominated by retail flows.However, analysts caution that the uptrend remains fragile. For Bitcoin to sustain upward momentum, it must maintain support above $70,000 while ETF inflows remain consistently positive.A breakdown below this level or a slowdown in institutional demand could expose the market to renewed volatility, particularly given the continued selling pressure from short-term holders.

data.title: Polygon Community Proposes New Fee Distribution Model
data.description:
The Polygon community has introduced Proposal PIP-85, which aims to revise the fee distribution model. According to Foresight News, the proposal suggests modifying the PIP-65 priority fee allocation formula by incorporating an equal-weight factor in validator distribution and setting a dividend ratio for stakers. It proposes extracting 50% from the validator pool to be distributed to stakers through a periodic Merkle claim program deployed on Ethereum. The remaining validator pool allocation would be adjusted to distribute 75% based on equal-weighted performance and 25% according to the existing staking weight formula.

Polygon Foundation CEO Sandeep Nailwal commented that the proposal offers a balanced approach to fairly reward all network participants, allowing stakers to directly receive fee income while ensuring the validator community remains healthy and incentivized.

data.title: Trump to Visit China in May for Xi Talks as Iran War Delays Add Geopolitical Tension
data.description:
Key Takeaways
U.S. President Donald Trump will visit China on May 14–15 for talks with President Xi Jinping.The trip was delayed due to the ongoing Iran conflict, adding pressure to U.S.-China relations.Markets are closely watching the meeting for signals on trade, geopolitics, and macro stability.Trump plans to host Xi in Washington later this year as part of reciprocal diplomacy.
U.S. President Donald Trump is set to visit China on May 14–15 for a high-level meeting with Xi Jinping, marking his first trip to Beijing in eight years and a key geopolitical event for global markets.
The visit, initially scheduled for earlier, was postponed due to the ongoing Iran conflict, which has introduced additional strain on U.S.-China relations and heightened uncertainty across risk assets, including the crypto market.
Trump confirmed the rescheduled trip in a Truth Social post, stating that preparations are underway for what he described as a “monumental” meeting. He also indicated plans to host Xi in Washington later this year, signaling an effort to stabilize ties between the world’s two largest economies.
The delay underscores the growing complexity of U.S. foreign policy, as Washington balances Middle East tensions with strategic competition in Asia. According to Trump’s spokesperson, Beijing understood the reason for the postponement.
While Chinese officials have not confirmed details of the visit, the meeting is expected to focus on trade, geopolitical coordination, and broader economic relations.
For markets, the talks could carry significant implications. Any progress in U.S.-China relations may ease global risk sentiment, while continued tensions could reinforce volatility across equities, commodities, and cryptocurrencies.
Investors will closely monitor the outcome for signals on trade policy, capital flows, and macro stability, all of which remain key drivers for Bitcoin and the broader crypto market.

data.title: Poland's Finance Minister Explores Fuel Price Reduction Strategies
data.description: Poland's Finance Minister, Domanski, has announced that the country is actively exploring strategies to reduce fuel prices. According to Jin10, this initiative comes as part of broader efforts to alleviate economic pressures on consumers and businesses. The government is considering various measures to address the rising costs of fuel, which have been a concern for both the public and the economy. Domanski emphasized the importance of finding effective solutions to ensure affordability and stability in the fuel market. The exploration of these strategies is ongoing, with the aim of implementing measures that will have a positive impact on the national economy.

data.title: TokenInsight Liquidity Report: Bitget Leads the BTC/ETH Contract Market
data.description: According to TokenInsight's "Cryptocurrency Exchange Liquidity Report," Binance maintains a leading position in order book depth for the BTC and ETH spot markets, significantly outperforming other platforms in the 0.03% and 0.05% ranges, followed by Bitget and OKX. In terms of slippage for large sell orders in the spot market, Binance maintains the lowest slippage for both BTC and ETH, while Bitget ranks second overall, demonstrating strong order book capacity. Regarding BTC bid-ask spreads, Binance and Bitget are in the optimal range. The contract market shows a differentiated pattern. Bitget excels in BTC and ETH contract order book depth, maintaining a leading position in the 0.05% and 0.1% ranges. In terms of slippage for large sell orders, BTC contract liquidity is relatively balanced among leading platforms, while ETH contracts show a more pronounced platform differentiation, with Bitget and OKX exhibiting the lowest slippage in the $5 million sell order scenario. Bid-ask spreads for BTC and ETH contracts remained low across major exchanges, reflecting the increasing maturity of the derivatives market structure. Regarding precious metal contracts, gold (XAU) and silver (XAG) exhibited different liquidity characteristics. Overall, Binance still dominated in terms of XAU and XAG contract depth, while Bitget maintained strong liquidity. In comparison, the XAU market had better overall depth, with slippage and spreads relatively controllable on leading platforms; while XAG showed higher slippage and wider spreads.
